_doc: Single source of truth for scripts/agent + scripts/agent-bench. Edit here, both consumers re-read on every invocation. The `compliant` regex is bench-only (Python re.match against the trimmed model output) — wrapper ignores it.

commit-msg.system: Generate a single-line conventional commit message for this git diff. Format: type(scope): description. Output ONLY the commit message line, no explanation, no preamble.
commit-msg.bench_prompt:
diff --git a/scripts/git-tracking.sh b/scripts/git-tracking.sh
@@ -1,5 +1,8 @@
+PLATFORM=$(git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | ...)
+echo "platform=$PLATFORM"
 diff --git a/config/openresty/lua/admin_api.lua b/config/openresty/lua/admin_api.lua
@@ -42,6 +42,9 @@ local function handle_agents()
+  local omlx = fetch_omlx_status()
+  resp.omlx_status = omlx

summarize-file.system: Summarize what this file does in ONE sentence. No preamble, no thinking. Output only the sentence.
summarize-file.bench_prompt:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
for f in $(find . -name '*.lua'); do
  luac -p "$f" || exit 1
done
echo 'all lua files compile'

classify-yesno.system: Reply with 'yes' or 'no' followed by max 10 words justification. Output only that line.
classify-yesno.bench_prompt: Is changing `if (x = null)` to `if (x == null)` a bug fix?

rename.system: Suggest 3 better variable names. Output as numbered list 1./2./3. with name and 3-word reason. Nothing else.

doc-comment.system: Write a one-line docstring for this function. Output only the docstring text, no quotes, no preamble.

extract-json.system: Extract structured data as a JSON object. Output ONLY the raw JSON. No markdown code fences, no backticks, no explanation. Start your response with `{`.
extract-json.bench_prompt:
192.168.1.42 - - [04/May/2026:12:33:01 +0000] "POST /api/login HTTP/1.1" 200 421
Extract fields: ip, method, status_code, path

explain-line.system: Explain in one short sentence what this code does. No preamble. Output only the sentence.
explain-line.bench_prompt: x = sum(1 for c in s if c.isalnum()) / max(len(s), 1)

refactor-suggestion.system: Suggest 1 specific refactor. Output: 1 sentence what to change + 1 sentence why. Nothing else.

ambiguity-check.system: If the request is too vague to answer confidently, reply 'AMBIGUOUS: <what is missing>'. Otherwise answer normally.

unix-recipe.system: Suggest 3 unix commands or one-liners that solve the task. Output as numbered list, no explanation.
unix-recipe.bench_prompt: Find all .lua files modified in the last 7 days, sorted by size

code-gen-short.system: Write code only. Your response MUST begin with `def`. No markdown code fences, no backticks, no explanation, no preamble of any kind.
code-gen-short.bench_prompt: Write a Python function `parse_size(s: str) -> int` that converts strings like '5KB', '2.5MB', '1.5GB' to bytes.

tldr.system: Summarize the input in ONE or TWO sentences. No bullet list. No preamble. Output only the summary.
tldr.bench_prompt: OpenResty is a web platform that integrates the standard Nginx core with LuaJIT, enabling developers to write Lua scripts that run inside Nginx workers. It is widely used for building high-performance API gateways, dynamic content servers, and microservice routers. Key advantages include co-routine-based concurrency, non-blocking I/O via cosockets, and a rich ecosystem of lua-resty-* libraries.

slug.system: Convert the input title to a URL-safe slug (lowercase, hyphens, no special chars, max 50 chars). Output ONLY the slug.
slug.bench_prompt: OMlx + GateII: A Local-First Approach to LLM Routing!

regex.system: Convert the natural-language description to a single regex pattern (PCRE-compatible, anchored). Output ONLY the regex on one line, no surrounding slashes, no explanation.
regex.bench_prompt: match a US ZIP code: 5 digits optionally followed by a hyphen and 4 more digits

error-explain.system: Explain in ONE short sentence what went wrong. Plain English, no jargon, no bullet list. Output only the sentence.
error-explain.bench_prompt: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length') at processItems (app.js:42:18)

keywords.system: Extract the top 5 keywords from the input. Output ONLY a valid JSON array where every element is a double-quoted string. Example: ["foo","bar","baz"]. Every element MUST use double quotes. No unquoted values. No markdown code fences, no backticks, no explanation. Start with `[`.
keywords.bench_prompt: OpenResty integrates Nginx with LuaJIT for high-performance, scriptable web applications, supporting non-blocking I/O via cosockets and co-routine concurrency.

reasoning.system: Solve the problem and output ONLY the final HH:MM answer. No explanation, no reasoning steps, no bold markdown, no other text. Just the time.
reasoning.bench_prompt: Train1 leaves 9am at 60km/h. Train2 leaves 10am same station, same direction, 90km/h. Meeting time?

tool-call-read.system: You have access to tools. Always use the provided tool to answer — do not answer from memory.
tool-call-read.bench_prompt: What is the content of the file /tmp/hello.txt?

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read the content of a file from disk

tool-call-bash.system: You have access to tools. Always use the provided tool to answer — do not answer from memory.
tool-call-bash.bench_prompt: Count the number of .lua files in /tmp.

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Run a bash shell command and return its stdout
